story_assessments.story_title: ChatGPT is acting more like a searchable work archive
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, freelancers, and small operators who keep drafts, briefs, notes, and reference material inside ChatGPT
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to find past work quickly so they can continue instead of rebuilding context from scratch
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI says ChatGPT can search across chats, projects, images, and documents on web, iOS, and Android, and is available again on WhatsApp in the EEA
story_assessments.stop_reason: The hook is a concrete workflow problem most creators recognize immediately: lost drafts and scattered context
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Faster recovery of old work and less restarting
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI says ChatGPT can search across chats, projects, images and documents
search_phrases: ChatGPT search across chats projects images documents
WhatsApp ChatGPT EEA voice notes image uploads
story_assessments.score_reason: Broad creator relevance, immediate practical value, and very easy to explain in a short social post.

story_assessments.story_title: Gemini Notebook is trying to make research feel like a workspace
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, researchers, and knowledge workers who build from source material and notes
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want research, source links, and rough ideas to stay connected so outlining is faster
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google says NotebookLM is now Gemini Notebook with a secure cloud computer, native code execution, and syncing across Gemini app and Google Search
story_assessments.stop_reason: Useful but slightly more niche and the rename may dilute instant recognition
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Less copying between tabs and a smoother move from reading to outlining
story_assessments.proof_point: Google says more than 30 million people and over 600,000 organizations use the product
search_phrases: secure cloud computer native code notebook
sync notebooks across Gemini app and Google Search
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong creator workflow angle, but it is less instantly legible than the archive or browser stories.

story_assessments.story_title: Study notebooks show how AI can teach, not just answer
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators who teach, coach, explain topics, or publish educational content
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want a way to turn messy source material into a structured learning path
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google says study notebooks generate diagnostic quizzes, personalised bite-sized lessons, practice quizzes, and a progress dashboard
story_assessments.stop_reason: The audience is useful but narrower, and the study framing is less broadly relevant to general creator workflows
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A better way to convert source notes into lessons, quizzes, or a clear next step
story_assessments.proof_point: Google says the feature is rolling out globally for personal accounts and soon for school-issued accounts
search_phrases: Gemini study notebooks quizzes lessons dashboard
turn source notes into a lesson outline
story_assessments.score_reason: Clear educational utility, but the fit is narrower than the other workflow stories.

story_assessments.story_title: Chrome is becoming a browser-side admin helper
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, freelancers, and solo operators who do research and admin in the browser
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to handle planning, email, scheduling, and research without constant app switching
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google says Gemini in Chrome is rolling out to desktop users in the U.K. and can summarize pages, compare tabs, draft emails in Gmail, schedule Calendar events, check Maps details, and ask questions about YouTube videos
story_assessments.stop_reason: Highly relatable browser workflow pain with clear day-to-day consequences
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Less tab-hopping and a more continuous work session inside one window
story_assessments.proof_point: Google says it includes prompt-injection safeguards and asks for confirmation before sensitive actions
search_phrases: Gemini in Chrome summarize pages compare tabs
draft emails in Gmail from Chrome
story_assessments.score_reason: Very practical and easy to picture, with strong value for creators who live in the browser.

tiktok.story_title: ChatGPT is acting more like a searchable work archive
tiktok.target_audience: Creators, freelancers, and small operators who keep drafts, briefs, notes, and reference material inside ChatGPT
tiktok.opening: Can your AI find the draft you already wrote?
tiktok.payoff: OpenAI says ChatGPT can search across chats, projects, images and documents on web, iOS and Android, which means less restarting and fewer lost notes.
tiktok.selection_reason: This is the most instantly relatable creator problem and it lands fast in a 35 to 55 word format.

youtube.story_title: Chrome is becoming a browser-side admin helper
youtube.target_audience: Creators, freelancers, and solo operators who do research and admin in the browser
youtube.opening: Would your browser handle the admin you keep postponing?
youtube.payoff: Google says Gemini in Chrome can summarize pages, compare tabs, draft emails in Gmail, schedule Calendar events, check Maps details, and ask questions about YouTube videos, with confirmation before sensitive actions.
youtube.selection_reason: YouTube Shorts can carry the fuller workflow explanation and show the day-to-day impact of browser-side AI.

facebook.story_title: ChatGPT is acting more like a searchable work archive
facebook.target_audience: Creators, freelancers, and small operators who keep drafts, briefs, notes, and reference material inside ChatGPT
facebook.opening: Can your AI find the draft you already wrote?
facebook.payoff: OpenAI says ChatGPT can search across chats, projects, images and documents on web, iOS and Android, and it is available again on WhatsApp in the EEA, so old briefs and references are easier to recover.
facebook.selection_reason: Facebook rewards familiar creator situations and a practical, conversational payoff.

pinterest.story_title: Gemini Notebook is trying to make research feel like a workspace
pinterest.target_audience: Creators, researchers, and knowledge workers who build from source material and notes
pinterest.opening: How do you keep research notes from scattering?
pinterest.payoff: Use Gemini Notebook to keep research, source links and rough ideas together so you can move from reading to outlining without rebuilding context.
pinterest.selection_reason: This story works well as a searchable, evergreen guide about keeping source material organized.

linkedin.story_title: Chrome is becoming a browser-side admin helper
linkedin.target_audience: Creators, freelancers, and solo operators who do research and admin in the browser
linkedin.opening: Is your browser already where your workday breaks?
linkedin.payoff: Google says Gemini in Chrome can summarize pages, compare tabs, draft emails in Gmail, schedule Calendar events, and check Maps details, with confirmation before sensitive actions, which can reduce context switching for research and admin.
linkedin.selection_reason: LinkedIn is the best fit for the operational lesson: workflow design, review discipline, and fewer context switches.

overall_reasoning: Story 0 and story 3 are the strongest audience-fit pieces because they solve immediate creator workflow problems: recovering old work and reducing browser-side admin. Story 1 is the best evergreen Pinterest fit because it reads like a reusable research guide. Story 2 is useful, but it is narrower and better suited to a teaching sub-audience than to the broadest social hooks. The selected platform mix keeps each post specific to how that feed tends to reward practical, searchable, human-led utility.
